rulings.Case Title: ALVIN RATLIFF VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, SEPTEMBER 5, 2007, LINE 1. DEFENDANT M. SLAYEN & ASSOCIATES, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT SHIFTED THE BURDEN OF PROOF UNDER ANDREWS. OCEGUEDA'S DECLARATION LACKS FOUNDATION. SINCE COHEN'S DECLARATION IS BASED IN PART ON OCEGUEDA, IT DOES NOT CREATE A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT. =(302/PJM/SN)

rulings.Case Title: ALVIN RATLIFF VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, SEPTEMBER 5, 2007, LINE 1. DEFENDANT FRASER'S BOILER SERVICE, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT SHIFTED THE BURDEN OF PROOF UNDER ANDREWS. OCEGUEDA'S DECLARATION LACKS FOUNDATION. COHEN'S DECLARATION IS BASED ON OCEGUEDA. DEFENDANT'S OBJECTIONS TO THE DECLARATIONS ARE SUSTAINED. PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO PRESENT TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: JEROME GROESHL et al VS. AC AND S, INC., et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, SEPTEMBER 5, 2007, LINE 14. DEFENDANT FOSTER WHEELER, L.L.C.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. NO OPPOSITION FILED. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: SAILORS UNION OF THE PACIFIC BUILDING CORPORATION VS. TERA NOVA INSURANCE COMPANY, A CORPORATION et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, SEPTEMBER 5, 2007, LINE 2. DEFENDANT TERRA NOVA INSURANCE COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. FAILED TO SHIFT BURDEN OF PROOF BECAUSE PLAINTIFF'S OBJECTIONS TO THE STRICKLER DECLARATION ARE GRANTED. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: ALVIN SYKES VS. DONNA BORLAND et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, SEPTEMBER 5, 2007, LINE 3. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION FOR DISCRIMINATION. NO CLAIM WAS FILED WTIN THE D.F.E.H. DECEDENT AND PLAINTIFF FAILED TO EXHAUST ADMINISTRATIVE REMEDIES. SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR RETALIATION: DECEDENT AND PLAINTIFF FAILED TO EXHAUST ADMINISTRATIVE REMEDIES. PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO PRESENT EVIDENCE OF RETALIATION. THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION FOR TERMINATION IN VIOLATION OF PUBLIC POLICY MAY NOT BE BROUGHT AGAINST A PUBLIC ENTITY. FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION BREACH OF STATUTORY DUTY. U. C. WAS NOT DECEDENT'S EMPLOYER. LABOR CODE SECTION 220 LIMIITS THE CITY'S LIABILITY FOR VIOLATIONS OF LABOR CODE SECTIONS 201 AND 203. PLAINTIFF'S THEFT CLAIM IS UNTIMELY. =(302/PJM/PM)

rulings.Case Title: RAYMOND WONG VS. TRI CAPITAL LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Tri Capital Llc, A California Limited Liability, Tri Acceptance Corporation'S Demurrer To Second Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, SEPTEMBER 5, 2007, LINE 15. DEFENDANTS TRI CAPITAL, L.L.C. AND TRI ACCEPTANCE CORPORATION'S DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED AS TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION. PLAINTIFF HAS NOT ALLEGED THAT THE TRI DEFENDANTS WERE A PARTY TO THE CONTRACT TO PURCHASE VICTORY VILLAGE LTD. III. OVERRULED AS TO THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR NEGLIGENT MISREPRESENTATION. HEARING TO DETERMINE WHETHER TO GRANT LEAVE TO AMEND. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: KENNETH MOREY VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Motion And Motion For Peremptory Writ Of Mandate
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, SEPTEMBER 5, 2007, LINE 11. PETITIONER KENNETH MOREY'S Motion For Peremptory Writ Of Mandate IS DENIED. IT IS PREMATURE TO DETERMINE WHETHER THE CITY HAS A MANDATORY DUTY TO DEFEND AND INDEMNIFY PETITIONER. ZOGRAFOS SEEKS PUNITIVE DAMAGES AND RECOVERY OF INTENTIONAL INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS. AS A CONSEQUENCE, THE ISSUE OF MALICE REMAINS TO BE DETERMINED. =(302/PJM/AY)

rulings.Case Title: FRANK BROWN et al VS. FAISAL HABEEB et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Hearing On Plaintiffs Frank Brown, Alvin H. Cardona, Galina J. Cardona'S Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, SEPTEMBER 5, 2007, LINE 13. PLAINTIFFS FRANK BROWN, ALVIN H. CARDONA AND GALINA J. CARDONA'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. THERE IS A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT AS TO THE AMOUT OF RENT DUE. =(302/PJM/JF)

rulings.Case Title: FRANK BROWN et al VS. FAISAL HABEEB et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Hearing On Defendant Faisal Habeeb'S Motion For Summary Judgment And Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, SEPTEMBER 5, 2007, LINE 13. DEFENDANT FAISAL HABEEB'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGEMENT, OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED. ALTHOUGH PLAINTIFF EXERCISED THE OPTION EARLY, THE LANDLORD WAIVED ANY OBJECTION THERETO BY CONDUCT. FURTHER, THE EARLY EXERCISE OF THE OPTION WAS NOT INCONSISTENT WITH THE PURPOSE AND INTENT OF THE RIGHT TO EXERCISE. =(302/PJM/JF)
